content: How did Samuel respond to the Lord's call at night?
explanation: 1 Samuel 3:10 - Eli taught Samuel how to respond to the Lord's voice. This shows readiness to listen and obey.
options: Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening

content: When choosing David as king, what lesson did the Lord teach Samuel?
explanation: 1 Samuel 16:7 - Man looks at the outward appearance, but the Lord looks at the heart. David was chosen because his heart was turned toward the Lord.
options: The Lord looks at the heart, not the outward appearance

content: What weapon did David use to defeat Goliath?
explanation: 1 Samuel 17:49-50 - David used a stone and a sling, trusting the Lord to fight the battle for him.

content: What did Hannah ask the Lord for at the tabernacle?
explanation: 1 Samuel 1:11 - Hannah prayed with tears and vowed to dedicate her son to the Lord. The Lord answered her prayer.
options: A son, vowing to dedicate him to the Lord

content: What wrong did Eli's two sons, Hophni and Phinehas, commit?
explanation: 1 Samuel 2:12-17 - They did not know the Lord, stole the offerings, and lived immorally. The consequence: both died on the same day.
options: Stole the offerings and lived immorally

content: What did the people of Israel ask Samuel for that grieved the Lord?
explanation: 1 Samuel 8:7 - The Lord said: They have not rejected you, but they have rejected me as their king. The people chose man over the Lord.
options: A king like the other nations

content: How is Saul, the first king of Israel, described?
explanation: 1 Samuel 9:2 - Saul had an impressive outward appearance, but his heart was not steadfast.
options: Taller than anyone else from the shoulders up, and handsome

content: What kind of person did Samuel say the Lord sought to replace Saul?
explanation: 1 Samuel 13:14 - The Lord sought a man after his own heart. David was called a man after God's own heart (Acts 13:22).
options: A man after God's own heart

content: Samuel said: "To obey is better than ___, and to listen is better than the fat of rams"
explanation: 1 Samuel 15:22 - The Lord desires obedience from the heart rather than outward religious formality.

content: The most famous friendship in the Bible is between whom?
explanation: 1 Samuel 18:1 - Jonathan loved David as himself. A faithful friendship that endured regardless of circumstances.

content: When he had the chance to kill Saul in the cave, what did David do?
explanation: 1 Samuel 24:5-7 - David honored Saul as the Lord's anointed, even though Saul sought to kill him. A lesson about respecting authority.
options: Only cut the corner of Saul's robe and was stricken with remorse for doing so

content: The second time David had the chance to kill Saul, what did he take instead of killing him?
explanation: 1 Samuel 26:11-12 - David continued to honor Saul's life, taking only the spear and water jug as proof.

content: In the battle with the Philistines at Aphek, what did Israel lose along with Eli's two sons?
explanation: 1 Samuel 4:11 — 'And the ark of God was captured, and the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, died.'

content: When the Philistines placed the Ark beside Dagon, what happened?
explanation: 1 Samuel 5:4 — 'Behold, Dagon had fallen face downward on the ground before the ark of the LORD, and the head of Dagon and both his hands were lying cut off on the threshold.'
options: Dagon fell face down; his head and hands were cut off

content: What does the stone Ebenezer set up by Samuel mean?
explanation: 1 Samuel 7:12 — 'Then Samuel took a stone... and called its name Ebenezer; for he said, Till now the LORD has helped us.'
options: Stone of help (Till now the LORD has helped us)

content: Whom did Samuel anoint as the first king of Israel?
explanation: 1 Samuel 10:1 — 'Then Samuel took a flask of oil and poured it on his head and kissed him and said, Has not the LORD anointed you to be prince over his people Israel?'

content: When Samuel anointed David, what came upon David from that day forward?
explanation: 1 Samuel 16:13 — 'Then Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him in the midst of his brothers. And the Spirit of the LORD rushed upon David from that day forward.'

content: What weapon did David use to defeat Goliath?
explanation: 1 Samuel 17:50 — 'So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and struck the Philistine and killed him. There was no sword in the hand of David.'

content: David blessed Abigail because she kept him from doing what?
explanation: 1 Samuel 25:32-33 — 'Blessed be the LORD... blessed be your discretion, and blessed be you, who have kept me this day from bloodguilt and from working salvation with my own hand.'
options: Bloodguilt and avenging by his own hand

content: On the night before his last battle, whom did Saul seek out to inquire?
explanation: 1 Samuel 28:7-8 — 'Then Saul said to his servants, Seek out for me a woman who is a medium... And his servants said, Behold, there is a medium at En-dor.'
